    • Education

Load initial script

Note that we exclude 164 twins/families with only a single twin in the analyses.

Code
source("0_load_data.R")

df = df %>% 
  filter(!(randomfamid %in% exclude_fams_onesib))

All measures used in study

Code
# Function to map variable prefix to study wave
get_study_wave = function(var_name) {
  prefix = substr(var_name, 1, 1)
  wave_map = c(
    "a" = "1st Contact",
    "b" = "2 Year",
    "c" = "3 Year",
    "d" = "4 Year",
    "e" = "In Home",
    "g" = "7 Year",
    "h" = "8 Year",
    "i" = "9 Year",
    "j" = "10 Year",
    "l" = "12 Year",
    "n" = "14 Year",
    "p" = "16 Year",
    "r" = "18 Year",
    "u" = "21 Year",
    "z" = "26 Year"
  )
  return(wave_map[prefix])
}

# Function to clean description labels
clean_description = function(x) {
  x %>%
    # Remove range info at end (e.g., ", 0-22", ", 1-9", ", 4-11")
    str_remove(", \\d+-\\d+$") %>%
    # Remove "see value labels"
    str_remove(", see value labels") %>%
    str_remove("see value labels") %>%
    # Spell out abbreviations inline
    str_replace("SOC employment", "Standard Occupational Classification employment") %>%
    str_replace("MHQ\\)", "Mental Health Questionnaire)") %>%
    str_replace("SLQ\\)", "questionnaire)") %>%
    str_replace("G-game", "G-game (general cognitive ability)") %>%
    str_replace_all(" qnr", " questionnaire") %>%
    # Clean up extra whitespace

    str_squish()
}

v_rq1x = 
  data.frame(
    `Short Label` = rq1x_labels_clean,
    Description = rq1x_labels,
    `Teds Code` = ifelse(rq1x %in% colnames(df0), rq1x,paste0(rq1x,"*")),
    `Range or Level` = sapply(rq1x, function(var) {
      if (class(df[[var]]) == "numeric") {
        paste0(round(min(df[[var]], na.rm = TRUE), 2), " — ", round(max(df[[var]], na.rm = TRUE), 2))
      } else if (is.factor(df[[var]])) {
        factor_levels = levels(df[[var]])
        paste(c(paste0(factor_levels[1],"*"), factor_levels[-1]), collapse = ", ")
      } else {
        paste(unique(df[[var]]), collapse = ", ")
      }
    }),
    N = sapply(rq1x, function(var) {
      sum(!is.na(df[[var]]))
    }),
    `Study Wave` = sapply(rq1x, get_study_wave)
  )

v_rq1x$Description = str_remove(v_rq1x$Description,"\\(1st.*")
v_rq1x$Description = clean_description(v_rq1x$Description)

# Manual edit for cens01pop98density study wave
v_rq1x$`Study.Wave`[v_rq1x$`Teds.Code` == "cens01pop98density"] = "1st Contact"
v_rq1x$`Study.Wave`[v_rq1x$`Teds.Code` == "pollution1998pca"] = "1st Contact"

# Clean uniform descriptions for participation indicator variables
rq1y_twin_descriptions = c(
  "Parent-report twin booklet data present",
  "Web test data present",
  "Self-report questionnaire data present",
  "Self-report behaviour booklet data present",
  "Self-report questionnaire data present",
  "TEDS21 phase 1 self-report questionnaire data present",
  "TEDS26 Mental Health Questionnaire data present",
  "CATSLife web test data present",
  "Genotyped data present"
)

v_rq1y =
  data.frame(
    `Short Label` = rq1y_twin_labels_clean,
    Description = rq1y_twin_descriptions,
    `Teds Code` = ifelse(rq1y_twin1 %in% colnames(df0), rq1y_twin1, paste0(rq1y_twin1, "*")),
    `Range or Level` = sapply(rq1y_twin1, function(var) {
      if (class(df[[var]]) == "numeric") {
        paste0(round(min(df[[var]], na.rm = TRUE), 2), " — ", round(max(df[[var]], na.rm = TRUE), 2))
      } else if (is.factor(df[[var]])) {
        factor_levels = levels(df[[var]])
        paste(c(paste0(factor_levels[1], "*"), factor_levels[-1]), collapse = ", ")
      } else {
        paste(unique(df[[var]]), collapse = ", ")
      }
    }),
    N = sapply(rq1y_twin1, function(var) {
      sum(!is.na(df[[var]]))
    }),
    `Study Wave` = sapply(rq1y_twin, get_study_wave)
  )

v_rq2 = data.frame(
  `Short Label` = rq2y_labels_short,
  Description   = rq2y_labels,
  `Teds Code` = ifelse(rq2y %in% colnames(df0), rq2y,paste0(rq2y,"*")),
  `Range or Level` = sapply(rq2y, function(var) {
    if (class(df[[var]]) == "numeric") {
      paste0(round(min(df[[var]], na.rm = TRUE), 2), " — ", round(max(df[[var]], na.rm = TRUE), 2))
    } else if (is.factor(df[[var]])) {
      factor_levels = levels(df[[var]])
      paste(c(paste0(factor_levels[1],"*"), factor_levels[-1]), collapse = ", ")
    } else {
      paste(unique(df[[var]]), collapse = ", ")
    }
  }),
  N = sapply(rq2y, function(var) {
    sum(!is.na(df[[var]]))
  }),
  `Study Wave` = sapply(rq2y, get_study_wave)
)

v_rq2$Description = str_remove(v_rq2$Description,"\\(2.*")

v_rq3 = data.frame(
  `Short Label` = rq6y_labels,
  Description   = clean_description(var_to_label(rq6y)),
  `Teds Code` = ifelse(rq6y %in% colnames(df0), rq6y, paste0(rq6y, "*")),
  `Range or Level` = sapply(rq6y, function(var) {
    if (class(df[[var]]) == "numeric") {
      paste0(round(min(df[[var]], na.rm = TRUE), 2), " — ", round(max(df[[var]], na.rm = TRUE), 2))
    } else if (is.factor(df[[var]])) {
      factor_levels = levels(df[[var]])
      paste(c(paste0(factor_levels[1], "*"), factor_levels[-1]), collapse = ", ")
    } else {
      paste(unique(df[[var]]), collapse = ", ")
    }
  }),
  N = sapply(rq6y, function(var) {
    sum(!is.na(df[[var]]))
  }),
  `Study Wave` = sapply(rq6y, get_study_wave)
)

# Combine all v_rq dataframes with row indices for grouping
v_rq1x_indexed = cbind(row_group = "RQ1 Variables", row_id = 1:nrow(v_rq1x), v_rq1x)
v_rq1y_indexed = cbind(row_group = "RQ1 Outcome Variables", row_id = (nrow(v_rq1x)+1):(nrow(v_rq1x)+nrow(v_rq1y)), v_rq1y)
v_rq2_indexed = cbind(row_group = "RQ2 Variables", row_id = (nrow(v_rq1x)+nrow(v_rq1y)+1):(nrow(v_rq1x)+nrow(v_rq1y)+nrow(v_rq2)), v_rq2)
v_rq3_indexed = cbind(row_group = "RQ3 Variables", row_id = (nrow(v_rq1x)+nrow(v_rq1y)+nrow(v_rq2)+1):(nrow(v_rq1x)+nrow(v_rq1y)+nrow(v_rq2)+nrow(v_rq3)), v_rq3)

v_rq_combined = rbind(v_rq1x_indexed, v_rq1y_indexed, v_rq2_indexed, v_rq3_indexed)

gt(v_rq_combined) %>%
  tab_row_group(
    label = "D) RQ2-3 Adolescence Measures",
    rows = row_group == "RQ3 Variables"
  ) %>%
  tab_row_group(
    label = "C) RQ2 Toddlerhood Measures",
    rows = row_group == "RQ2 Variables"
  ) %>%
  tab_row_group(
    label = "B) RQ1-2 Participation Indicators (1 = yes, 0 = no)",
    rows = row_group == "RQ1 Outcome Variables"
  ) %>%
  tab_row_group(
    label = "A) Baseline variables used to predict later participation, and for IP weighting",
    rows = row_group == "RQ1 Variables"
  ) %>%
  cols_hide(c(row_group, row_id)) %>%
  cols_width(
    `Range.or.Level` ~ px(100)
  ) %>%
  cols_label_with(fn = ~ gsub("\\.", " ", .x)) %>%
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(font = "Times New Roman", size = px(12)),
    locations = cells_body()
  ) %>%
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(font = "Times New Roman", size = px(12)),
    locations = cells_column_labels()
  ) %>%
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(font = "Times New Roman", size = px(12)),
    locations = cells_row_groups()
  ) %>%
  tab_footnote(
    footnote = "Note. MFQ = Mood and Feelings Questionnaire; SDQ = Strengths and Difficulties Questionnaire; GCSE = General Certificate of Secondary Education; KS3 = Key Stage 3; GAD-D = Generalized Anxiety Disorder scale; PARCA = Parent Report of Children's Abilities; MZ = Monozygotic; DZ = Dizygotic; HNC = Higher National Certificate; HND = Higher National Diploma; CSE = Certificate of Secondary Education."
  )
Short Label Description Teds Code Range or Level N Study Wave
A) Baseline variables used to predict later participation, and for IP weighting
Twin Sex Twin sex, 0female 1male sex1 0 — 1 25876 NA
Mother age at birth Age in years of natural mother at time of birth of twins amumagetw 14 — 45 25402 1st Contact
Father age at birth Age in years of natural father at time of birth of twins adadagetw 16 — 66 22836 1st Contact
Single Parent Single Parent asingle cohabiting biological mother and father / cohabiting biological parent with other*, single parent 25428 1st Contact
Zygosity Twin pair zygosity (best available estimate), 1MZ 2DZ zygos 1*, 2 25876 26 Year
Mother medical risk Mother medical risk factor composite scale amedtot -2.49 — 4.98 25864 1st Contact
Father employment level Father Standard Occupational Classification employment level afasoc2* 1*, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, caring for children at home, no job 22966 1st Contact
Father education level Male parent highest qualification level afahqual CSE grade 1 or O-level/GCSE grade A-C*, no qualifications, CSE grade 2-5 or O-level/GCSE grade D-G, A-level or S-level, HNC, HND, undergraduate degree, postgraduate qualification 22902 1st Contact
Mother employment level Mother Standard Occupational Classification employment level amosoc2* caring for children at home*, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, no job 25486 1st Contact
Mother education level Female parent highest qualification level amohqual no qualifications*, CSE grade 2-5 or O-level/GCSE grade D-G, CSE grade 1 or O-level/GCSE grade A-C, A-level or S-level, HNC, HND, undergraduate degree, postgraduate qualification 25466 1st Contact
Twin medical risk Twin medical risk factor composite scale atwmed1 -1.88 — 3.33 25832 1st Contact
Ethnic origin Ethnic origin of twins, original codes aethnicc White*, Asian, Black, Mixed race, Other 25782 1st Contact
Language at home Main language spoken at home alang other*, English, English + other 25516 1st Contact
Older siblings Number of older siblings anoldsib 0*, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 or more 25876 1st Contact
Younger siblings Number of younger siblings anyngsib 0*, 1, 2 or more 25876 1st Contact
Twins club member Member of a Twins Club atwclub 0*, 1 24990 1st Contact
Childcare by others Twins looked after by anyone else alookels 0*, 1 24636 1st Contact
Smoking in pregnancy Smoked cigarettes while pregnant asmoke 0*, 1 25768 1st Contact
Alcohol in pregnancy Drank alcohol while pregnant adrink 0*, 1 25620 1st Contact
Severe stress in pregnancy Severe stress during pregnancy astress 0*, 1 25726 1st Contact
Pollution index Principal Component of 1998 pollution variables pollution1998pca -2.47 — 4.57 22884 1st Contact
B) RQ1-2 Participation Indicators (1 = yes, 0 = no)
Y4 (parent-report twin booklet) Parent-report twin booklet data present dtwdata1 0 — 1 25876 4 Year
Y12 (web tests) Web test data present lcwdata1 0 — 1 25876 12 Year
Y12 (questionnaire) Self-report questionnaire data present lcqdata1 0 — 1 25876 12 Year
Y16 (behaviour booklet) Self-report behaviour booklet data present pcbhdata1 0 — 1 25876 16 Year
Y18 (questionnaire) Self-report questionnaire data present rcqdata1 0 — 1 25876 18 Year
Y21 (TEDS21 phase-1 questionnaire) TEDS21 phase 1 self-report questionnaire data present u1cdata1 0 — 1 25876 21 Year
Y26 (TEDS26 questionnaire) TEDS26 Mental Health Questionnaire data present zmhdata1 0 — 1 25876 26 Year
Y26 (CATSLife web tests) CATSLife web test data present zcdata1 0 — 1 25876 26 Year
Genotyped Genotyped data present hasgeno1 1, 0, NA 23780 8 Year
C) RQ2 Toddlerhood Measures
Maternal Education Maternal Education (formatted as numeric variable) amohqualn1 1 — 8 25466 1st Contact
Vocabulary Vocabulary total score bvocab1 0 — 100 11742 2 Year
Grammar Grammar composite score bgramma1 0 — 2 11695 2 Year
Parent-admin cognition Parent-administered Parca mean score badparn1 -3.09 — 3.45 11677 2 Year
Parent-report cognition Parent-reported Parca total score breparc1 0 — 26 11784 2 Year
Conduct problems Conduct SDQ-comparable Behar subscale bsdqccont1 0 — 8 11728 2 Year
Emotional problems Emotion SDQ-comparable Behar subscale bsdqcemot1 0 — 4 11735 2 Year
Hyperactivity Hyperactivity SDQ-comparable Behar subscale bsdqchypt1 0 — 6 11748 2 Year
Peer problems Peer SDQ-comparable Behar subscale bsdqcpert1 0 — 6 11478 2 Year
Prosocial behavior Prosocial SDQ-comparable Behar subscale bsdqcprot1 0 — 10 11642 2 Year
D) RQ2-3 Adolescence Measures
Y12: Depression (MFQ) MFQ scale from 11 MFQ items (child self-report) at 12 lcmfqt1 0 — 22 11403 12 Year
Y12: Externalising (SDQ) SDQ Externalising scale at 12 lsdqext1 0 — 20 11360 12 Year
Y12: Cognitive ability G composite scale from child web tests at 12, standardised lcg1 -3.67 — 3.04 8444 12 Year
Y16: GCSE grade Core subjects (English, maths, science): mean grade in GCSE results (twin exams at 16) pcexgcsecoregrdm1 4 — 11 12942 16 Year
Note. MFQ = Mood and Feelings Questionnaire; SDQ = Strengths and Difficulties Questionnaire; GCSE = General Certificate of Secondary Education; KS3 = Key Stage 3; GAD-D = Generalized Anxiety Disorder scale; PARCA = Parent Report of Children's Abilities; MZ = Monozygotic; DZ = Dizygotic; HNC = Higher National Certificate; HND = Higher National Diploma; CSE = Certificate of Secondary Education.

Participation-Related plots

Missing Data at each timepoint

Participation Rates

Code
df %>%
  select(cohort, all_of(c("acontact",rq1y_twin1))) %>%
  group_by(cohort) %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), 
                   ~ length(which(. == 1)) / length(which(. >= 0)),
                   .names = "{.col}"),
            .groups = "keep") %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -cohort, names_to = "Time Point", values_to = "Participation Rate") %>%
  mutate(
    `Time Point` = factor(`Time Point`, 
                              levels = c("acontact",rq1y_twin1), 
                              labels = c("Baseline",rq1y_twin_labels_clean_extrashort)),
    cohort = gsub(" twins", "",cohort),
    cohort = gsub(" to ", "-",cohort)
    ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = `Time Point`, y = `Participation Rate`, color = cohort, group = cohort)) + 
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(round(`Participation Rate` * 100, 1), "%")),
    vjust = -0.5,
    hjust = 0.5,
    size = 3,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(), limits = c(0,1)) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(x = "Time Point", 
       y = "Participation Rate", 
       color = "Cohort",
       title = "Participation Rates Over Time by Cohort") +
  theme(legend.position = c(.8,.8),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) + 
  theme(plot.margin = margin(t = 1, r = 1, b = 1, l = 10, unit = "mm")) 
Warning: A numeric `legend.position` argument in `theme()` was deprecated in ggplot2
3.5.0.
ℹ Please use the `legend.position.inside` argument of `theme()` instead.
Code
df %>%
  select(all_of(c("acontact",rq1y_twin1))) %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), 
                   ~ length(which(. == 1)) / length(which(. >= 0)),
                   .names = "{.col}"),
            .groups = "keep") %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = everything(), names_to = "Time Point", values_to = "Participation Rate") %>%
  mutate(
    `Time Point` = factor(`Time Point`, 
                              levels = c("acontact",rq1y_twin1), 
                              labels = c("Baseline",rq1y_twin_labels_clean_extrashort))
    ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = `Time Point`, y = `Participation Rate`, group = 1)) + 
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(round(`Participation Rate` * 100, 1), "%")),
    col = "#2c3e50",
    vjust = 3,
    hjust = 0.5,
    size = 3,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(), limits = c(0,1)) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(x = "Time Point", 
       y = "Participation Rate", 
       color = "Cohort",
       title = "Participation Rates Over Time (Twin-level participation)") +
  theme(legend.position = c(.8,.8),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) + 
  theme(plot.margin = margin(t = 1, r = 1, b = 1, l = 10, unit = "mm"))
Code
save_plot("11_participation_rates_twinlevel", width = 6, height = 4)

View full-size plot

Twin Concordance

NOTE THAT WE REMOVE COHORTS 2 & 3

ALSO FOR CORRELATIONS - I SHOULD FILTER BY TWIN == 1!

Code
vars_to_na = c("btwdata","ctwdata", "gcdata","icdata","jcdata","pcwebdata")

df_cohort34_removed = df %>%
  mutate(
      across(starts_with(vars_to_na), ~ ifelse(cohort %in% c("Cohort 3: twins born Sep-95 to Aug-96","Cohort 4: twins born Sep-96 to Dec-96"), NA, .))
    )

plot_data = map_dfr(rq1y_twin, function(var){
  df_cohort34_removed %>% 
    group_by(sexzyg) %>%
    summarise(
      mean = mean(.data[[paste0(var,"1")]], na.rm = TRUE),
      cor  = cor(x=.data[[paste0(var,"1")]], y=.data[[paste0(var,"2")]], use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
    # cor  = {
    #   group_data <- cur_data()
    #   as.numeric(psych::tetrachoric(x=group_data[paste0(var,1:2)])$rho)[2]
    # }
    ) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    mutate(variable = var)
}, .id = "var_index") %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(mean, cor), 
               names_to = "metric", 
               values_to = "value") %>%
  mutate(
    metric_label = case_when(
      metric == "mean" ~ "Participation Rate",
      metric == "cor" ~ "Twin Tetrachoric Correlation"
    ),
    time_point = rq1y_twin_labels_clean_extrashort[match(.$variable, rq1y_twin)],
    time_point = factor(time_point, levels = rq1y_twin_labels_clean_extrashort)
  )

plot_data %>%
  ggplot( aes(x = time_point, y = value, color = sexzyg, group = sexzyg)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5, alpha = 0.9) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(aes(label = case_when(
    metric == "mean" ~ paste0(round(value * 100, 1), "%"),
    metric == "cor" ~ gbtoolbox::apa_num(value)
  )),
  position = position_dodge(width = 0.5),
  vjust = 2,
  size = 2,
  show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~metric_label, scales = "free_y", ncol = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "MZ male" = "#d7191c",
      "DZ male" = "#fdae61",
      "MZ female" = "#2c7bb6",
      "DZ female" = "#abd9e9",
      "DZ opposite sexes" = "grey"
    )
  ) +
  ggh4x::scale_y_facet(metric_label == "Participation Rate",
                       labels = scales::percent_format(),
                       breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.1)) +
  labs(
    # title = "Twin Study: Participation Rates and Correlations Across Time Points",
    # subtitle = "Trends by Zygosity and Sex",
    x = "Participation Time Point",
    y = NULL,
    color = "Zygosity",
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    # plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    # plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12),
    # strip.text = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
    legend.spacing.y = unit(0, "pt"),
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = alpha("white",.5), color = NA),
    legend.key.height = unit(0.6, "pt"),
    legend.text = element_text(margin = margin(t = 0, b = 0)),
    legend.position = c(0.02, 0.02),
    legend.justification = c(0, 0),
    # legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1),
    plot.margin = margin(t = 5.5, r = 5.5, b = 5.5, l = 47, unit = "pt")
  ) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3)))
Code
save_plot("11_participation_rates_zygosity", width = 7, height = 6)

View full-size plot

Create ACE estimates plot

Code
estimates = readRDS(file.path("results","1_ace_estimates.Rds"))
ci       = readRDS(file.path("results","1_ace_confints.Rds"))

estimates %>%
    mutate(
      par_clean = case_when(
        par == "a1" ~ "A",
        par == "c1" ~ "C",
        par == "e1" ~ "E"
      ),
      time_point = rq1y_twin_labels_clean[match(name, rq1y_twin)],
      time_point = factor(time_point, levels = rq1y_twin_labels_clean)
    ) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = time_point, y = value, color = par_clean, group = interaction(sex, par_clean))) +
    geom_line(linewidth = .5, alpha = 0.8, aes(linetype = sex)) +
    geom_point(size = 2.5, alpha = 0.9, aes(shape = sex)) +
    # ggrepel::geom_text_repel(aes(label = sprintf("%.2f", value)),
    #   size = 1.5,
    #   show.legend = FALSE,
    #   min.segment.length = 0,
    #   segment.size = 0.2,
    #   max.overlaps = Inf,
    #   direction = "y",
    #   force = 2) +
    scale_color_manual(
      values = c(
        "A" = "#d7191c",
        "C" = "#fdae61", 
        "E" = "#2c7bb6"
      )
    ) +
    scale_linetype_manual(
      values = c("male" = "solid", "female" = "dotted")
    ) +
    scale_shape_manual(
      values = c("male" = 16, "female" = 17)
    ) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::number_format(accuracy = 0.01)) +
    labs(
      x = "Participation Time Point",
      y = NULL,
      color = "ACE Component",
      linetype = "Sex",
      shape = "Sex"
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      legend.background = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "top",
      legend.justification = "center",
      legend.direction = "horizontal",
      legend.box = "horizontal",
      legend.key.height = unit(0.5, "lines"),
      legend.key.width = unit(0.8, "lines"),
      legend.text = element_text(size = 8),
      legend.title = element_text(size = 9),
      legend.margin = margin(2, 2, 2, 2),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1),
      plot.margin = margin(t = 5.5, r = 5.5, b = 5.5, l = 47, unit = "pt"),
      plot.title = element_text(size = 8, hjust = 0.5),
      strip.text = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold")
    ) +
    guides(
      color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 2), order = 1),
      linetype = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 0.8), order = 2),
      shape = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 1.5), order = 2)
    )
Warning: Duplicated `override.aes` is ignored.
Code
  save_plot("11_ACE_time", width = 7, height = 3)
Warning: Duplicated `override.aes` is ignored.
Duplicated `override.aes` is ignored.

View full-size plot

ACE Estimates Table

Code
var_labels_vec = setNames(rq1y_twin_labels_clean, rq1y_twin)

ace_gt_data = ci %>%
  mutate(
    par_clean = case_when(
      par == "a1" ~ "A",
      par == "c1" ~ "C",
      par == "e1" ~ "E"
    ),
    time_point = var_labels_vec[variable],
    time_point = factor(time_point, levels = rq1y_twin_labels_clean)
  ) %>%
  select(time_point, sex, par_clean, est, lower, upper) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from  = par_clean,
    values_from = c(est, lower, upper),
    names_glue  = "{par_clean}_{.value}"
  ) %>%
  arrange(sex, time_point) %>%
  mutate(sex = tools::toTitleCase(sex))

ace_gt_data %>%
  gt(groupname_col = "sex") %>%
  tab_spanner(label = "A (Genetic)",          columns = starts_with("A_")) %>%
  tab_spanner(label = "C (Shared Env.)",       columns = starts_with("C_")) %>%
  tab_spanner(label = "E (Non-shared Env.)",   columns = starts_with("E_")) %>%
  cols_label(
    time_point = "Time Point",
    A_est = "Est.", A_lower = "Lower", A_upper = "Upper",
    C_est = "Est.", C_lower = "Lower", C_upper = "Upper",
    E_est = "Est.", E_lower = "Lower", E_upper = "Upper"
  ) %>%
  fmt_number(columns = where(is.numeric), decimals = 3) %>%
  sub_missing(missing_text = "\u2014") %>%
  tab_header(
    title    = "ACE Estimates for Participation Indicators",
    subtitle = "Point estimates with 95% confidence intervals"
  ) %>%
  tab_footnote(
    footnote = "Note. A = additive genetic; C = shared environment; E = non-shared environment. Values are variance components."
  )
ACE Estimates for Participation Indicators
Point estimates with 95% confidence intervals
Time Point
A (Genetic)
C (Shared Env.)
E (Non-shared Env.)

ANOVA Sex & Zygosity predicting participation

Code
map_dfr(seq_along(rq1y_twin1), function(i){
  y_var     = rq1y_twin1[i]
  model     = glm(paste(y_var, "~ zygos + sex1"), data = df, family = binomial)
  robust_se = clubSandwich::coef_test(model, cluster = df$randomfamid, vcov = "CR0", test = "z")
  
  data.frame(
    `Time Point`    = rq1y_twin_labels_clean[i],
    `Zygosity Coef` = as.numeric(robust_se[2, "beta"]),
    `Zygosity p`    = as.numeric(robust_se[2, "p_z"]),
    `Sex Coef`      = as.numeric(robust_se[3, "beta"]),
    `Sex p`         = as.numeric(robust_se[3, "p_z"])
  )
}) %>%
  knitr::kable(digits = 3)
Registered S3 method overwritten by 'clubSandwich':
  method    from    
  bread.mlm sandwich
Time.Point Zygosity.Coef Zygosity.p Sex.Coef Sex.p
Y4 (parent-report twin booklet) -0.064 0.091 -0.100 0.001
Y12 (web tests) -0.233 0.000 -0.240 0.000
Y12 (questionnaire) -0.218 0.000 -0.175 0.000
Y16 (behaviour booklet) -0.176 0.000 -0.337 0.000
Y18 (questionnaire) -0.162 0.000 -0.224 0.000
Y21 (TEDS21 phase-1 questionnaire) -0.196 0.000 -0.753 0.000
Y26 (TEDS26 questionnaire) -0.194 0.000 -0.878 0.000
Y26 (CATSLife web tests) -0.259 0.000 -0.794 0.000
Genotyped -0.766 0.000 -0.176 0.000

How many different time points do we have for each participant (excluding first contact)

Code
df %>%
  select(all_of(rq1y_twin1)) %>%
  apply(., 1, function(x) sum(x, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% 
  tibble(`Number Time Points Participated` = .) %>% 
  count(`Number Time Points Participated`) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = `Number Time Points Participated`, y = n)) + 
  geom_col() + 
  geom_text(aes(label = n), vjust = 1.2, color = "white", size = 3.5) +
  theme_bw() + 
  labs(
    title = "How many times has each pps been tested (excl. first contact)?",
    subtitle = "Twin-level participation numbers (N = 27890)"
    )
Code
save_plot("11_number_of_times_each_twin_participated", width = 9, height = 5)

View full-size plot

How predictive are cohort and year of birth of participation at each timepoint?

I’ve checked there are no missing values for cohort or year of birth (aonsby)

View full-size plot

Code
models = list()

for (i in seq_along(rq1y_twin1)){
  # cat(i, "/", length(rq1y),"\n")
  formula <- as.formula(paste(rq1y_twin1[i], "~ cohort + aonsby"))
  models[[i]] = glm(formula, data = df, family = binomial)
}

var_explained = sapply(models, function(x) as.numeric(performance::r2(x)))

cbind.data.frame(rq1y_twin1, rq1y_twin_labels_clean, var_explained) %>%
  `colnames<-`(c("Var","Label","R2 by Cohort and Birth Year")) %>%
  knitr::kable(digits = 2)
Var Label R2 by Cohort and Birth Year
dtwdata1 Y4 (parent-report twin booklet) 0.01
lcwdata1 Y12 (web tests) 0.00
lcqdata1 Y12 (questionnaire) 0.00
pcbhdata1 Y16 (behaviour booklet) 0.01
rcqdata1 Y18 (questionnaire) 0.05
u1cdata1 Y21 (TEDS21 phase-1 questionnaire) 0.00
zmhdata1 Y26 (TEDS26 questionnaire) 0.00
zcdata1 Y26 (CATSLife web tests) 0.00
hasgeno1 Genotyped 0.00
Code
cbind.data.frame(rq1y_twin1, rq1y_twin_labels_clean, var_explained) %>%
  `colnames<-`(c("Var","Label","Variance Explained")) %>%
  mutate(
    Label = factor(Label, levels = rq1y_twin_labels_clean)
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=Label,y=`Variance Explained`)) + 
  geom_col() + 
  geom_text(aes(label = gbtoolbox::apa_num(`Variance Explained`)),
            vjust = 1.1, color = "white", size = 3) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust=1, vjust=1)) +
  scale_x_discrete(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.1, 0)))
Code
save_plot("11_r2_participation_cohort_birthyear", width = 9, height = 5)

View full-size plot

Cohort alone is not helpful for determining who was eligible at t2 and t3

As we can see below, nobody in cohort 4 took part in Y2, Y3, Y10 data collection. However, a minority of participants took part in Y2-Y3 from cohort 3, but participation rates are quite low suggesting that some participants were eligible and some were not.

Code
 table(df0$cohort, df0$btwoyear)
   
       0    1
  1 1866 4664
  2 3900 5876
  3 6558 1978
  4 3048    0

       0    1
  1 2348 4182
  2 3838 5938
  3 6622 1914
  4 3048    0

  3 8536    0
  4 3048    0

Descriptive stats on specific variables

Availability of genotyped data by sex/zygosity and ethnicity

Note that below, the double entered twin variable genotyped1/2 indicates the presence (1) or absence (0) of genotyped data (see data dictionary).

For the analyses, I created the variables geno1/2 which sets the value to 1 if one has an MZ twin that has been genotyped.

Sex/Zygosity

Code
df %>%
    split(.$sexzyg) %>%
    map(~ table(.x$genotyped1, .x$genotyped2))

Age of twins at each time point

View full-size plot

Plot

Code
df_age = df %>%
  select(contains("age")) %>%
  select(-contains("genpro"), -contains("mumage"), - contains("dadage"), -ends_with("2")) 

df_age_long = df_age %>%
  `colnames<-`(paste0(var_to_label(colnames(df_age)), " (", colnames(df_age), ")")) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = everything())

df_age_long %>% 
  mutate(name = factor(name, levels = var_to_label(colnames(df_age)))) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=value)) + 
  geom_histogram(bins = 100) +
  facet_wrap(~name, ncol =3) + 
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0,30,by=2)) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank()
    )
Warning: Removed 200874 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range
(`stat_bin()`).
Code
save_plot("11_participant_ages_each_timepoint", width = 12, height = 12)
Warning: Removed 200874 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range
(`stat_bin()`).
Removed 200874 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range
(`stat_bin()`).

View full-size plot

Table

Code
df_age_long %>%
  mutate(name = factor(name, levels = paste0(var_to_label(colnames(df_age)), " (", colnames(df_age), ")"))) %>%
  group_by(name) %>%
  summarise(
    n = sum(!is.na(value)),
    mean = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd = sd(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    min = min(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    p10 = quantile(value, 0.10, na.rm = TRUE),
    p90 = quantile(value, 0.90, na.rm = TRUE),
    max = max(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_label(
    name = "Timepoint",
    n = "N",
    mean = "Mean",
    sd = "SD",
    min = "Min",
    p10 = "10th",
    p90 = "90th",
    max = "Max"
  ) %>%
  fmt_number(
    columns = c(mean, sd),
    decimals = 2
  ) %>%
  fmt_number(
    columns = c(min, p10, p90, max),
    decimals = 1
  ) %>%
  fmt_number(
    columns = n,
    use_seps = TRUE,
    decimals = 0
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Participant Age at Each Timepoint"
  ) %>%
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = cells_column_labels()
  )
Participant Age at Each Timepoint
Timepoint N Mean SD Min 10th 90th Max

Education

Code
all(table(df$amohqual)==table(df$amohqualn))
[1] TRUE
Code
df %>%
  filter(twin == 1) %>%
  count(amohqual) %>%
  mutate(
    numeric_code = as.numeric(amohqual),
    percent = n / sum(n)
  ) %>%
  select(numeric_code, amohqual, n, percent) %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_label(
    numeric_code = "Code",
    amohqual = "Education Level",
    n = "N",
    percent = "Percent"
  ) %>%
  fmt_percent(columns = percent)
Code Education Level N Percent
1 no qualifications 1198 9.26%
2 CSE grade 2-5 or O-level/GCSE grade D-G 1989 15.37%
3 CSE grade 1 or O-level/GCSE grade A-C 4754 36.74%
4 A-level or S-level 1693 13.09%
5 HNC 356 2.75%
6 HND 484 3.74%
7 undergraduate degree 1484 11.47%
8 postgraduate qualification 775 5.99%
NA NA 205 1.58%

Relationship between maternal education and child outcomes

In some analyses, maternal education is treated as a numeric variable (1-8), which assumes a linear relationship with child outcomes. To check whether this assumption is reasonable, we examine the predicted child education & cognition outcomes at each maternal education level.

We regress child outcomes on maternal education using dummy coding (i.e., treating each education level as a separate category). The plots below show the predicted outcome for each education level. If the linearity assumption holds, these points should fall approximately on a straight line.

We find that mothers with A Levels have generally similar child outcomes to those with Higher National Certificate and Higher National Diploma qualifications. Thus, the assumption of linearity seems unreasonable, and it might be problematic to have these two categories as separate numeric codes.

Code
# Helper function to create marginal effects plot
plot_education_effects = function(model, y_label) {
  marginaleffects::predictions(model, by = "amohqual") %>%
    data.frame() %>%
    rename(
      prediction = estimate,
      education_level = amohqual
    ) %>%
    ggplot(aes(y = prediction, x = education_level, group = 1)) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high), width = 0.2) +
    geom_point(size = 2) +
    geom_line() +
    labs(
      x = "Maternal Education Level",
      y = y_label
    ) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1)
    )
}

KS3 Academic Achievement (Age 14)

Code
model_ks3 = df %>%
  filter(twin == 1) %>%
  filter(!is.na(amohqual)) %>%
  lm(npks3tall1 ~ amohqual, data = .)

summary(model_ks3)

Call:

Residuals:
    Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
-4.8343 -0.4258  0.0387  0.4676  3.4833

                                                Estimate Std. Error t value

amohqualA-level or S-level                       0.78212    0.07622  10.261
amohqualHNC                                      0.66686    0.10411   6.405
amohqualHND                                      0.66195    0.09343   7.085
amohqualundergraduate degree                     0.97547    0.07645  12.759
amohqualpostgraduate qualification               1.01003    0.08398  12.028
                                                Pr(>|t|)    
(Intercept)                                      < 2e-16 ***
amohqualCSE grade 2-5 or O-level/GCSE grade D-G 0.000462 ***
amohqualCSE grade 1 or O-level/GCSE grade A-C   5.36e-14 ***
amohqualA-level or S-level                       < 2e-16 ***
amohqualHNC                                     1.77e-10 ***
amohqualHND                                     1.77e-12 ***
amohqualundergraduate degree                     < 2e-16 ***
amohqualpostgraduate qualification               < 2e-16 ***

  (10038 observations deleted due to missingness)

lm(formula = pcexgcsecoregrdm1 ~ amohqual, data = .)

Residuals:
    Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
-4.8199 -0.6750  0.0801  0.8085  3.0983 

Coefficients:
                                                Estimate Std. Error t value
(Intercept)                                      7.90170    0.06447 122.557
amohqualCSE grade 2-5 or O-level/GCSE grade D-G  0.28982    0.07583   3.822
amohqualCSE grade 1 or O-level/GCSE grade A-C    0.77326    0.06831  11.319
amohqualA-level or S-level                       1.21822    0.07365  16.540
amohqualHNC                                      1.14144    0.10074  11.331
amohqualHND                                      1.21801    0.09240  13.182
amohqualundergraduate degree                     1.74640    0.07336  23.806
amohqualpostgraduate qualification               1.79888    0.08084  22.253
                                                Pr(>|t|)    
(Intercept)                                      < 2e-16 ***
amohqualCSE grade 2-5 or O-level/GCSE grade D-G 0.000134 ***
amohqualCSE grade 1 or O-level/GCSE grade A-C    < 2e-16 ***
amohqualA-level or S-level                       < 2e-16 ***
amohqualHNC                                      < 2e-16 ***
amohqualHND                                      < 2e-16 ***
amohqualundergraduate degree                     < 2e-16 ***
amohqualpostgraduate qualification               < 2e-16 ***

  (6317 observations deleted due to missingness)

F-statistic: 204.6 on 7 and 6408 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
Code
plot_education_effects(model_gcse, "Predicted GCSE Score")

save_plot("11_meducation_gcse_plot", width = 6, height = 5)

Cognitive Ability (Age 12)

Code

  filter(twin == 1) %>%
  filter(!is.na(amohqual)) %>%
  lm(lcg1 ~ amohqual, data = .)

summary(model_cog)

Example of attritioning for presentations

Code
df %>%
  filter(!is.na(lsdqext1)) %>%
  mutate(lsdqext1_attritioned = ifelse(zcdata1==1, lsdqext1, NA)) %>%
  select(lsdqext1, lsdqext1_attritioned) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = everything()) %>%
  mutate(
    name = factor(ifelse(name=="lsdqext1", "Original", "Attritioned (Y26 Web Test)"),
                  levels = c("Original", "Attritioned (Y26 Web Test)"))
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = value, fill = name)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 12, color = "white", linewidth = 0.2) +
  facet_wrap(~name, ncol = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Original" = "#4C72B0", "Attritioned (Y26 Web Test)" = "#DD8452")) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(x = "Year 2 Externalising Symptoms", y = "Count") +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey90"),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
Warning: Removed 8428 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range
(`stat_bin()`).
Code
save_plot("11_attritioning_example", width  = 4, height = 6)
Warning: Removed 8428 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range (`stat_bin()`).
Removed 8428 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range (`stat_bin()`).

View full-size plot

Source Code
---
title: "Descriptive Information"
format: 
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    fig-width: 8
    fig-height: 8
    code-link: true
    code-fold: true
    code-tools: true
    df-print: paged
    toc: true
    grid:
      body-width: 900px
editor: source
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

# Load initial script

Note that we exclude 164 twins/families with only a single twin in the analyses. 

```{r}
#| label: load-data
#| output: false

source("0_load_data.R")

df = df %>% 
  filter(!(randomfamid %in% exclude_fams_onesib)) 

```

# All measures used in study

```{r}
#| label: variable-tables

# Function to map variable prefix to study wave
get_study_wave = function(var_name) {
  prefix = substr(var_name, 1, 1)
  wave_map = c(
    "a" = "1st Contact",
    "b" = "2 Year",
    "c" = "3 Year",
    "d" = "4 Year",
    "e" = "In Home",
    "g" = "7 Year",
    "h" = "8 Year",
    "i" = "9 Year",
    "j" = "10 Year",
    "l" = "12 Year",
    "n" = "14 Year",
    "p" = "16 Year",
    "r" = "18 Year",
    "u" = "21 Year",
    "z" = "26 Year"
  )
  return(wave_map[prefix])
}

# Function to clean description labels
clean_description = function(x) {
  x %>%
    # Remove range info at end (e.g., ", 0-22", ", 1-9", ", 4-11")
    str_remove(", \\d+-\\d+$") %>%
    # Remove "see value labels"
    str_remove(", see value labels") %>%
    str_remove("see value labels") %>%
    # Spell out abbreviations inline
    str_replace("SOC employment", "Standard Occupational Classification employment") %>%
    str_replace("MHQ\\)", "Mental Health Questionnaire)") %>%
    str_replace("SLQ\\)", "questionnaire)") %>%
    str_replace("G-game", "G-game (general cognitive ability)") %>%
    str_replace_all(" qnr", " questionnaire") %>%
    # Clean up extra whitespace

    str_squish()
}

v_rq1x = 
  data.frame(
    `Short Label` = rq1x_labels_clean,
    Description = rq1x_labels,
    `Teds Code` = ifelse(rq1x %in% colnames(df0), rq1x,paste0(rq1x,"*")),
    `Range or Level` = sapply(rq1x, function(var) {
      if (class(df[[var]]) == "numeric") {
        paste0(round(min(df[[var]], na.rm = TRUE), 2), " — ", round(max(df[[var]], na.rm = TRUE), 2))
      } else if (is.factor(df[[var]])) {
        factor_levels = levels(df[[var]])
        paste(c(paste0(factor_levels[1],"*"), factor_levels[-1]), collapse = ", ")
      } else {
        paste(unique(df[[var]]), collapse = ", ")
      }
    }),
    N = sapply(rq1x, function(var) {
      sum(!is.na(df[[var]]))
    }),
    `Study Wave` = sapply(rq1x, get_study_wave)
  )

v_rq1x$Description = str_remove(v_rq1x$Description,"\\(1st.*")
v_rq1x$Description = clean_description(v_rq1x$Description)

# Manual edit for cens01pop98density study wave
v_rq1x$`Study.Wave`[v_rq1x$`Teds.Code` == "cens01pop98density"] = "1st Contact"
v_rq1x$`Study.Wave`[v_rq1x$`Teds.Code` == "pollution1998pca"] = "1st Contact"

# Clean uniform descriptions for participation indicator variables
rq1y_twin_descriptions = c(
  "Parent-report twin booklet data present",
  "Web test data present",
  "Self-report questionnaire data present",
  "Self-report behaviour booklet data present",
  "Self-report questionnaire data present",
  "TEDS21 phase 1 self-report questionnaire data present",
  "TEDS26 Mental Health Questionnaire data present",
  "CATSLife web test data present",
  "Genotyped data present"
)

v_rq1y =
  data.frame(
    `Short Label` = rq1y_twin_labels_clean,
    Description = rq1y_twin_descriptions,
    `Teds Code` = ifelse(rq1y_twin1 %in% colnames(df0), rq1y_twin1, paste0(rq1y_twin1, "*")),
    `Range or Level` = sapply(rq1y_twin1, function(var) {
      if (class(df[[var]]) == "numeric") {
        paste0(round(min(df[[var]], na.rm = TRUE), 2), " — ", round(max(df[[var]], na.rm = TRUE), 2))
      } else if (is.factor(df[[var]])) {
        factor_levels = levels(df[[var]])
        paste(c(paste0(factor_levels[1], "*"), factor_levels[-1]), collapse = ", ")
      } else {
        paste(unique(df[[var]]), collapse = ", ")
      }
    }),
    N = sapply(rq1y_twin1, function(var) {
      sum(!is.na(df[[var]]))
    }),
    `Study Wave` = sapply(rq1y_twin, get_study_wave)
  )

v_rq2 = data.frame(
  `Short Label` = rq2y_labels_short,
  Description   = rq2y_labels,
  `Teds Code` = ifelse(rq2y %in% colnames(df0), rq2y,paste0(rq2y,"*")),
  `Range or Level` = sapply(rq2y, function(var) {
    if (class(df[[var]]) == "numeric") {
      paste0(round(min(df[[var]], na.rm = TRUE), 2), " — ", round(max(df[[var]], na.rm = TRUE), 2))
    } else if (is.factor(df[[var]])) {
      factor_levels = levels(df[[var]])
      paste(c(paste0(factor_levels[1],"*"), factor_levels[-1]), collapse = ", ")
    } else {
      paste(unique(df[[var]]), collapse = ", ")
    }
  }),
  N = sapply(rq2y, function(var) {
    sum(!is.na(df[[var]]))
  }),
  `Study Wave` = sapply(rq2y, get_study_wave)
)

v_rq2$Description = str_remove(v_rq2$Description,"\\(2.*")

v_rq3 = data.frame(
  `Short Label` = rq6y_labels,
  Description   = clean_description(var_to_label(rq6y)),
  `Teds Code` = ifelse(rq6y %in% colnames(df0), rq6y, paste0(rq6y, "*")),
  `Range or Level` = sapply(rq6y, function(var) {
    if (class(df[[var]]) == "numeric") {
      paste0(round(min(df[[var]], na.rm = TRUE), 2), " — ", round(max(df[[var]], na.rm = TRUE), 2))
    } else if (is.factor(df[[var]])) {
      factor_levels = levels(df[[var]])
      paste(c(paste0(factor_levels[1], "*"), factor_levels[-1]), collapse = ", ")
    } else {
      paste(unique(df[[var]]), collapse = ", ")
    }
  }),
  N = sapply(rq6y, function(var) {
    sum(!is.na(df[[var]]))
  }),
  `Study Wave` = sapply(rq6y, get_study_wave)
)

# Combine all v_rq dataframes with row indices for grouping
v_rq1x_indexed = cbind(row_group = "RQ1 Variables", row_id = 1:nrow(v_rq1x), v_rq1x)
v_rq1y_indexed = cbind(row_group = "RQ1 Outcome Variables", row_id = (nrow(v_rq1x)+1):(nrow(v_rq1x)+nrow(v_rq1y)), v_rq1y)
v_rq2_indexed = cbind(row_group = "RQ2 Variables", row_id = (nrow(v_rq1x)+nrow(v_rq1y)+1):(nrow(v_rq1x)+nrow(v_rq1y)+nrow(v_rq2)), v_rq2)
v_rq3_indexed = cbind(row_group = "RQ3 Variables", row_id = (nrow(v_rq1x)+nrow(v_rq1y)+nrow(v_rq2)+1):(nrow(v_rq1x)+nrow(v_rq1y)+nrow(v_rq2)+nrow(v_rq3)), v_rq3)

v_rq_combined = rbind(v_rq1x_indexed, v_rq1y_indexed, v_rq2_indexed, v_rq3_indexed)

gt(v_rq_combined) %>%
  tab_row_group(
    label = "D) RQ2-3 Adolescence Measures",
    rows = row_group == "RQ3 Variables"
  ) %>%
  tab_row_group(
    label = "C) RQ2 Toddlerhood Measures",
    rows = row_group == "RQ2 Variables"
  ) %>%
  tab_row_group(
    label = "B) RQ1-2 Participation Indicators (1 = yes, 0 = no)",
    rows = row_group == "RQ1 Outcome Variables"
  ) %>%
  tab_row_group(
    label = "A) Baseline variables used to predict later participation, and for IP weighting",
    rows = row_group == "RQ1 Variables"
  ) %>%
  cols_hide(c(row_group, row_id)) %>%
  cols_width(
    `Range.or.Level` ~ px(100)
  ) %>%
  cols_label_with(fn = ~ gsub("\\.", " ", .x)) %>%
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(font = "Times New Roman", size = px(12)),
    locations = cells_body()
  ) %>%
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(font = "Times New Roman", size = px(12)),
    locations = cells_column_labels()
  ) %>%
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(font = "Times New Roman", size = px(12)),
    locations = cells_row_groups()
  ) %>%
  tab_footnote(
    footnote = "Note. MFQ = Mood and Feelings Questionnaire; SDQ = Strengths and Difficulties Questionnaire; GCSE = General Certificate of Secondary Education; KS3 = Key Stage 3; GAD-D = Generalized Anxiety Disorder scale; PARCA = Parent Report of Children's Abilities; MZ = Monozygotic; DZ = Dizygotic; HNC = Higher National Certificate; HND = Higher National Diploma; CSE = Certificate of Secondary Education."
  )

```

# Participation-Related plots

## Missing Data at each timepoint 

#### Participation Rates

```{r}
#| label: participation-rates

df %>%
  select(cohort, all_of(c("acontact",rq1y_twin1))) %>%
  group_by(cohort) %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), 
                   ~ length(which(. == 1)) / length(which(. >= 0)),
                   .names = "{.col}"),
            .groups = "keep") %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -cohort, names_to = "Time Point", values_to = "Participation Rate") %>%
  mutate(
    `Time Point` = factor(`Time Point`, 
                              levels = c("acontact",rq1y_twin1), 
                              labels = c("Baseline",rq1y_twin_labels_clean_extrashort)),
    cohort = gsub(" twins", "",cohort),
    cohort = gsub(" to ", "-",cohort)
    ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = `Time Point`, y = `Participation Rate`, color = cohort, group = cohort)) + 
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(round(`Participation Rate` * 100, 1), "%")),
    vjust = -0.5,
    hjust = 0.5,
    size = 3,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(), limits = c(0,1)) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(x = "Time Point", 
       y = "Participation Rate", 
       color = "Cohort",
       title = "Participation Rates Over Time by Cohort") +
  theme(legend.position = c(.8,.8),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) + 
  theme(plot.margin = margin(t = 1, r = 1, b = 1, l = 10, unit = "mm")) 

df %>%
  select(all_of(c("acontact",rq1y_twin1))) %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), 
                   ~ length(which(. == 1)) / length(which(. >= 0)),
                   .names = "{.col}"),
            .groups = "keep") %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = everything(), names_to = "Time Point", values_to = "Participation Rate") %>%
  mutate(
    `Time Point` = factor(`Time Point`, 
                              levels = c("acontact",rq1y_twin1), 
                              labels = c("Baseline",rq1y_twin_labels_clean_extrashort))
    ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = `Time Point`, y = `Participation Rate`, group = 1)) + 
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(round(`Participation Rate` * 100, 1), "%")),
    col = "#2c3e50",
    vjust = 3,
    hjust = 0.5,
    size = 3,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(), limits = c(0,1)) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(x = "Time Point", 
       y = "Participation Rate", 
       color = "Cohort",
       title = "Participation Rates Over Time (Twin-level participation)") +
  theme(legend.position = c(.8,.8),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) + 
  theme(plot.margin = margin(t = 1, r = 1, b = 1, l = 10, unit = "mm")) 

save_plot("11_participation_rates_twinlevel", width = 6, height = 4)

```

[View full-size plot](plots/pngs/11_participation_rates_twinlevel.png){target="_blank"}

#### Twin Concordance

NOTE THAT WE REMOVE COHORTS 2 & 3

ALSO FOR CORRELATIONS - I SHOULD FILTER BY TWIN == 1! 

```{r}
#| label: twin-concordance

vars_to_na = c("btwdata","ctwdata", "gcdata","icdata","jcdata","pcwebdata")

df_cohort34_removed = df %>%
  mutate(
      across(starts_with(vars_to_na), ~ ifelse(cohort %in% c("Cohort 3: twins born Sep-95 to Aug-96","Cohort 4: twins born Sep-96 to Dec-96"), NA, .))
    )

plot_data = map_dfr(rq1y_twin, function(var){
  df_cohort34_removed %>% 
    group_by(sexzyg) %>%
    summarise(
      mean = mean(.data[[paste0(var,"1")]], na.rm = TRUE),
      cor  = cor(x=.data[[paste0(var,"1")]], y=.data[[paste0(var,"2")]], use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
    # cor  = {
    #   group_data <- cur_data()
    #   as.numeric(psych::tetrachoric(x=group_data[paste0(var,1:2)])$rho)[2]
    # }
    ) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    mutate(variable = var)
}, .id = "var_index") %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(mean, cor), 
               names_to = "metric", 
               values_to = "value") %>%
  mutate(
    metric_label = case_when(
      metric == "mean" ~ "Participation Rate",
      metric == "cor" ~ "Twin Tetrachoric Correlation"
    ),
    time_point = rq1y_twin_labels_clean_extrashort[match(.$variable, rq1y_twin)],
    time_point = factor(time_point, levels = rq1y_twin_labels_clean_extrashort)
  )

plot_data %>%
  ggplot( aes(x = time_point, y = value, color = sexzyg, group = sexzyg)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5, alpha = 0.9) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(aes(label = case_when(
    metric == "mean" ~ paste0(round(value * 100, 1), "%"),
    metric == "cor" ~ gbtoolbox::apa_num(value)
  )),
  position = position_dodge(width = 0.5),
  vjust = 2,
  size = 2,
  show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~metric_label, scales = "free_y", ncol = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "MZ male" = "#d7191c",
      "DZ male" = "#fdae61",
      "MZ female" = "#2c7bb6",
      "DZ female" = "#abd9e9",
      "DZ opposite sexes" = "grey"
    )
  ) +
  ggh4x::scale_y_facet(metric_label == "Participation Rate",
                       labels = scales::percent_format(),
                       breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.1)) +
  labs(
    # title = "Twin Study: Participation Rates and Correlations Across Time Points",
    # subtitle = "Trends by Zygosity and Sex",
    x = "Participation Time Point",
    y = NULL,
    color = "Zygosity",
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    # plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    # plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12),
    # strip.text = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
    legend.spacing.y = unit(0, "pt"),
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = alpha("white",.5), color = NA),
    legend.key.height = unit(0.6, "pt"),
    legend.text = element_text(margin = margin(t = 0, b = 0)),
    legend.position = c(0.02, 0.02),
    legend.justification = c(0, 0),
    # legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1),
    plot.margin = margin(t = 5.5, r = 5.5, b = 5.5, l = 47, unit = "pt")
  ) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3)))

save_plot("11_participation_rates_zygosity", width = 7, height = 6)


```

[View full-size plot](plots/pngs/11_participation_rates_zygosity.png){target="_blank"}

#### Create ACE estimates plot 

```{r}
#| label: ace-estimates-plot
estimates = readRDS(file.path("results","1_ace_estimates.Rds"))
ci       = readRDS(file.path("results","1_ace_confints.Rds"))

estimates %>%
    mutate(
      par_clean = case_when(
        par == "a1" ~ "A",
        par == "c1" ~ "C",
        par == "e1" ~ "E"
      ),
      time_point = rq1y_twin_labels_clean[match(name, rq1y_twin)],
      time_point = factor(time_point, levels = rq1y_twin_labels_clean)
    ) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = time_point, y = value, color = par_clean, group = interaction(sex, par_clean))) +
    geom_line(linewidth = .5, alpha = 0.8, aes(linetype = sex)) +
    geom_point(size = 2.5, alpha = 0.9, aes(shape = sex)) +
    # ggrepel::geom_text_repel(aes(label = sprintf("%.2f", value)),
    #   size = 1.5,
    #   show.legend = FALSE,
    #   min.segment.length = 0,
    #   segment.size = 0.2,
    #   max.overlaps = Inf,
    #   direction = "y",
    #   force = 2) +
    scale_color_manual(
      values = c(
        "A" = "#d7191c",
        "C" = "#fdae61", 
        "E" = "#2c7bb6"
      )
    ) +
    scale_linetype_manual(
      values = c("male" = "solid", "female" = "dotted")
    ) +
    scale_shape_manual(
      values = c("male" = 16, "female" = 17)
    ) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::number_format(accuracy = 0.01)) +
    labs(
      x = "Participation Time Point",
      y = NULL,
      color = "ACE Component",
      linetype = "Sex",
      shape = "Sex"
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      legend.background = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "top",
      legend.justification = "center",
      legend.direction = "horizontal",
      legend.box = "horizontal",
      legend.key.height = unit(0.5, "lines"),
      legend.key.width = unit(0.8, "lines"),
      legend.text = element_text(size = 8),
      legend.title = element_text(size = 9),
      legend.margin = margin(2, 2, 2, 2),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1),
      plot.margin = margin(t = 5.5, r = 5.5, b = 5.5, l = 47, unit = "pt"),
      plot.title = element_text(size = 8, hjust = 0.5),
      strip.text = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold")
    ) +
    guides(
      color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 2), order = 1),
      linetype = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 0.8), order = 2),
      shape = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 1.5), order = 2)
    )

  save_plot("11_ACE_time", width = 7, height = 3)

```

[View full-size plot](plots/pngs/11_ACE_time.png){target="_blank"}

#### ACE Estimates Table

```{r}
#| label: ace-estimates-table

var_labels_vec = setNames(rq1y_twin_labels_clean, rq1y_twin)

ace_gt_data = ci %>%
  mutate(
    par_clean = case_when(
      par == "a1" ~ "A",
      par == "c1" ~ "C",
      par == "e1" ~ "E"
    ),
    time_point = var_labels_vec[variable],
    time_point = factor(time_point, levels = rq1y_twin_labels_clean)
  ) %>%
  select(time_point, sex, par_clean, est, lower, upper) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from  = par_clean,
    values_from = c(est, lower, upper),
    names_glue  = "{par_clean}_{.value}"
  ) %>%
  arrange(sex, time_point) %>%
  mutate(sex = tools::toTitleCase(sex))

ace_gt_data %>%
  gt(groupname_col = "sex") %>%
  tab_spanner(label = "A (Genetic)",          columns = starts_with("A_")) %>%
  tab_spanner(label = "C (Shared Env.)",       columns = starts_with("C_")) %>%
  tab_spanner(label = "E (Non-shared Env.)",   columns = starts_with("E_")) %>%
  cols_label(
    time_point = "Time Point",
    A_est = "Est.", A_lower = "Lower", A_upper = "Upper",
    C_est = "Est.", C_lower = "Lower", C_upper = "Upper",
    E_est = "Est.", E_lower = "Lower", E_upper = "Upper"
  ) %>%
  fmt_number(columns = where(is.numeric), decimals = 3) %>%
  sub_missing(missing_text = "\u2014") %>%
  tab_header(
    title    = "ACE Estimates for Participation Indicators",
    subtitle = "Point estimates with 95% confidence intervals"
  ) %>%
  tab_footnote(
    footnote = "Note. A = additive genetic; C = shared environment; E = non-shared environment. Values are variance components."
  )

```


#### ANOVA Sex & Zygosity predicting participation

```{r}
#| label: anova-sex-zygosity

map_dfr(seq_along(rq1y_twin1), function(i){
  y_var     = rq1y_twin1[i]
  model     = glm(paste(y_var, "~ zygos + sex1"), data = df, family = binomial)
  robust_se = clubSandwich::coef_test(model, cluster = df$randomfamid, vcov = "CR0", test = "z")
  
  data.frame(
    `Time Point`    = rq1y_twin_labels_clean[i],
    `Zygosity Coef` = as.numeric(robust_se[2, "beta"]),
    `Zygosity p`    = as.numeric(robust_se[2, "p_z"]),
    `Sex Coef`      = as.numeric(robust_se[3, "beta"]),
    `Sex p`         = as.numeric(robust_se[3, "p_z"])
  )
}) %>%
  knitr::kable(digits = 3)

```


## How many different time points do we have for each participant (excluding first contact)

```{r}
#| label: timepoints-per-participant

df %>%
  select(all_of(rq1y_twin1)) %>%
  apply(., 1, function(x) sum(x, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% 
  tibble(`Number Time Points Participated` = .) %>% 
  count(`Number Time Points Participated`) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = `Number Time Points Participated`, y = n)) + 
  geom_col() + 
  geom_text(aes(label = n), vjust = 1.2, color = "white", size = 3.5) +
  theme_bw() + 
  labs(
    title = "How many times has each pps been tested (excl. first contact)?",
    subtitle = "Twin-level participation numbers (N = 27890)"
    )

save_plot("11_number_of_times_each_twin_participated", width = 9, height = 5)

```

[View full-size plot](plots/pngs/11_number_of_times_each_twin_participated.png){target="_blank"}

## How predictive are cohort and year of birth of participation at each timepoint?

I've checked there are no missing values for cohort or year of birth (aonsby)

[View full-size plot](plots/11_r2_participation_cohort_birthyear.pdf){target="_blank"}

```{r}
#| label: cohort-birthyear-models
models = list()

for (i in seq_along(rq1y_twin1)){
  # cat(i, "/", length(rq1y),"\n")
  formula <- as.formula(paste(rq1y_twin1[i], "~ cohort + aonsby"))
  models[[i]] = glm(formula, data = df, family = binomial)
}

var_explained = sapply(models, function(x) as.numeric(performance::r2(x)))

cbind.data.frame(rq1y_twin1, rq1y_twin_labels_clean, var_explained) %>%
  `colnames<-`(c("Var","Label","R2 by Cohort and Birth Year")) %>%
  knitr::kable(digits = 2)


cbind.data.frame(rq1y_twin1, rq1y_twin_labels_clean, var_explained) %>%
  `colnames<-`(c("Var","Label","Variance Explained")) %>%
  mutate(
    Label = factor(Label, levels = rq1y_twin_labels_clean)
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=Label,y=`Variance Explained`)) + 
  geom_col() + 
  geom_text(aes(label = gbtoolbox::apa_num(`Variance Explained`)),
            vjust = 1.1, color = "white", size = 3) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust=1, vjust=1)) +
  scale_x_discrete(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.1, 0)))

save_plot("11_r2_participation_cohort_birthyear", width = 9, height = 5)


```

[View full-size plot](plots/pngs/11_r2_participation_cohort_birthyear.png){target="_blank"}

At the Y18 time point, we see that those in cohort 1 and those born in 1994 are less likely to participate. 

```{r}
#| label: model-summary

rq1y_twin_labels_clean[5]

summary(models[[5]])

```

### Cohort alone is not helpful for determining who was eligible at t2 and t3

As we can see below, nobody in cohort 4 took part in Y2, Y3, Y10 data collection. However, a minority of participants took part in Y2-Y3 from cohort 3, but participation rates are quite low suggesting that some participants were eligible and some were not.

```{r}
#| label: cohort-eligibility-cohort

 table(df0$cohort, df0$btwoyear)
 table(df0$cohort, df0$cthreeyr)
 table(df0$cohort, df0$jtenyear)

```


```{r}
#| label: cohort-eligibility-birthyear

 table(df0$AONSBY, df0$btwoyear)
 table(df0$AONSBY, df0$cthreeyr)
 table(df0$AONSBY, df0$jtenyear)

```

## Participation Patterns

```{r}
#| label: participation-patterns

df %>%
  select(all_of(rq1y_twin1)) %>%
  rename_with(~var_to_label(., df)) %>%
  mutate_all(~na_if(., 0)) %>%
  ggmice::plot_pattern(npat = 70, rotate = TRUE) + 
  scale_x_discrete(limits = (var_to_label(rq1y_twin1))) + 
  coord_flip()

save_plot("11_participation_pattern", width = 19, height = 6)


# df %>%
#   select(all_of(rq1y_twin1)) %>%
#   mutate_all(~ .==1) %>%
#   rowwise() %>%

#     `Year 2 - 4 present`   = any(btwoyear, cthreeyr, dfouryr),
#     `Year 7 - 12 present`  = any(gsevenyr, heightyr, jtenyear),
#     `Year 14 - 16 present` = any(n14year, p16year),
#     `Year 18 - 26 present` = any(rcqdata, uteds21data, zmhdata)
#   ) %>%
#   select(ends_with("present")) %>%
#   mutate_all(~ na_if(., FALSE)) %>%
#   ggmice::plot_pattern(rotate = TRUE) + 
#   scale_x_discrete(limits = c("Year 2 - 4 present", "Year 7 - 12 present", "Year 14 - 16 present", "Year 18 - 26 present")) + 
#   coord_flip()
# 
# save_plot("11_participation_pattern_grouped", width = 19, height = 6)

df %>%
  select(all_of(rq1y_twin1)) %>%
  `colnames<-`((rq1y_twin_labels_clean_extrashort)) %>% 
  mutate_all(~ na_if(., 0)) %>%
  gbtoolbox::plot_pairwise_missing(textadjust = 2.5) +
  labs(subtitle = "Number of twins tested at each time point (diagonal) and \npair of time points (below diagonal)")

save_plot("11_pairwise_missingness_plot", width = 6, height = 6)

```

[View full-size plot](plots/pngs/11_participation_pattern.png){target="_blank"}

[View full-size plot](plots/pngs/11_pairwise_missingness_plot.png){target="_blank"}

# Descriptive stats on specific variables

## Availability of genotyped data by sex/zygosity and ethnicity

Note that below, the double entered twin variable `genotyped1/2` indicates the presence (1) or absence (0) of genotyped data (see [data dictionary](https://datadictionary.teds.ac.uk/studies/variable_lists/background_variables.htm)). 

For the analyses, I created the variables `geno1/2` which sets the value to 1 if one has an MZ twin that has been genotyped. 

Sex/Zygosity

```{r}
#| label: genotyped-sex-zygosity

df %>%
    split(.$sexzyg) %>%
    map(~ table(.x$genotyped1, .x$genotyped2))

df %>%
    split(.$sexzyg) %>%
    map(~ table(.x$hasgeno1, .x$hasgeno2))

```

Ethnicity

```{r}
#| label: genotyped-ethnicity
df %>%
    group_by(aethnicc) %>%
    summarise(
      `N genotyped` = length(which(genotyped1 == 1)),
      `% genotyped` = length(which(genotyped1 == 1))/dplyr::n()
    ) %>% 
  gt() %>% 
  fmt_percent(columns = 3)

```



## Age of twins at each time point

[View full-size plot](plots/11_participant_ages_each_timepoint.pdf){target="_blank"}

### Plot

```{r}
#| label: age-plot

df_age = df %>%
  select(contains("age")) %>%
  select(-contains("genpro"), -contains("mumage"), - contains("dadage"), -ends_with("2")) 

df_age_long = df_age %>%
  `colnames<-`(paste0(var_to_label(colnames(df_age)), " (", colnames(df_age), ")")) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = everything())

df_age_long %>% 
  mutate(name = factor(name, levels = var_to_label(colnames(df_age)))) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=value)) + 
  geom_histogram(bins = 100) +
  facet_wrap(~name, ncol =3) + 
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0,30,by=2)) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank()
    )

save_plot("11_participant_ages_each_timepoint", width = 12, height = 12)


```

[View full-size plot](plots/pngs/11_participant_ages_each_timepoint.png){target="_blank"}

### Table

```{r}
#| label: age-table

df_age_long %>%
  mutate(name = factor(name, levels = paste0(var_to_label(colnames(df_age)), " (", colnames(df_age), ")"))) %>%
  group_by(name) %>%
  summarise(
    n = sum(!is.na(value)),
    mean = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd = sd(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    min = min(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    p10 = quantile(value, 0.10, na.rm = TRUE),
    p90 = quantile(value, 0.90, na.rm = TRUE),
    max = max(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_label(
    name = "Timepoint",
    n = "N",
    mean = "Mean",
    sd = "SD",
    min = "Min",
    p10 = "10th",
    p90 = "90th",
    max = "Max"
  ) %>%
  fmt_number(
    columns = c(mean, sd),
    decimals = 2
  ) %>%
  fmt_number(
    columns = c(min, p10, p90, max),
    decimals = 1
  ) %>%
  fmt_number(
    columns = n,
    use_seps = TRUE,
    decimals = 0
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Participant Age at Each Timepoint"
  ) %>%
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = cells_column_labels()
  )

```

## Education

```{r}
#| label: education-table

all(table(df$amohqual)==table(df$amohqualn))

df %>%
  filter(twin == 1) %>%
  count(amohqual) %>%
  mutate(
    numeric_code = as.numeric(amohqual),
    percent = n / sum(n)
  ) %>%
  select(numeric_code, amohqual, n, percent) %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_label(
    numeric_code = "Code",
    amohqual = "Education Level",
    n = "N",
    percent = "Percent"
  ) %>%
  fmt_percent(columns = percent)


```

## Relationship between maternal education and child outcomes

In some analyses, maternal education is treated as a numeric variable (1-8), which assumes a linear relationship with child outcomes. To check whether this assumption is reasonable, we examine the predicted child education & cognition outcomes at each maternal education level.

We regress child outcomes on maternal education using dummy coding (i.e., treating each education level as a separate category). The plots below show the predicted outcome for each education level. If the linearity assumption holds, these points should fall approximately on a straight line.

We find that mothers with A Levels have generally similar child outcomes to those with Higher National Certificate and Higher National Diploma qualifications. Thus, the assumption of linearity seems unreasonable, and it might be problematic to have these two categories as separate numeric codes. 

```{r}
#| label: education-effects-function

# Helper function to create marginal effects plot
plot_education_effects = function(model, y_label) {
  marginaleffects::predictions(model, by = "amohqual") %>%
    data.frame() %>%
    rename(
      prediction = estimate,
      education_level = amohqual
    ) %>%
    ggplot(aes(y = prediction, x = education_level, group = 1)) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high), width = 0.2) +
    geom_point(size = 2) +
    geom_line() +
    labs(
      x = "Maternal Education Level",
      y = y_label
    ) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1)
    )
}

```

### KS3 Academic Achievement (Age 14)

```{r}
#| label: ks3-model

model_ks3 = df %>%
  filter(twin == 1) %>%
  filter(!is.na(amohqual)) %>%
  lm(npks3tall1 ~ amohqual, data = .)

summary(model_ks3)

plot_education_effects(model_ks3, "Predicted KS3 Score")

save_plot("11_meducation_ks3_plot", width = 6, height = 5)

```

[View full-size plot](plots/pngs/11_meducation_ks3_plot.png){target="_blank"}

### GCSE Core Subjects Grade (Age 16)

```{r}
#| label: gcse-model

model_gcse = df %>%
  filter(twin == 1) %>%
  filter(!is.na(amohqual)) %>%
  lm(pcexgcsecoregrdm1 ~ amohqual, data = .)

summary(model_gcse)

plot_education_effects(model_gcse, "Predicted GCSE Score")

save_plot("11_meducation_gcse_plot", width = 6, height = 5)

```

[View full-size plot](plots/pngs/11_meducation_gcse_plot.png){target="_blank"}

### Cognitive Ability (Age 12)

```{r}
#| label: cognitive-model

model_cog = df %>%
  filter(twin == 1) %>%
  filter(!is.na(amohqual)) %>%
  lm(lcg1 ~ amohqual, data = .)

summary(model_cog)

plot_education_effects(model_cog, "Predicted Cognitive Score (g)")

save_plot("11_meducation_cognitive_plot", width = 6, height = 5)

```

[View full-size plot](plots/pngs/11_meducation_cognitive_plot.png){target="_blank"}

# Example of attritioning for presentations


```{r}
#| label: attritioning-example

df %>%
  filter(!is.na(lsdqext1)) %>%
  mutate(lsdqext1_attritioned = ifelse(zcdata1==1, lsdqext1, NA)) %>%
  select(lsdqext1, lsdqext1_attritioned) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = everything()) %>%
  mutate(
    name = factor(ifelse(name=="lsdqext1", "Original", "Attritioned (Y26 Web Test)"),
                  levels = c("Original", "Attritioned (Y26 Web Test)"))
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = value, fill = name)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 12, color = "white", linewidth = 0.2) +
  facet_wrap(~name, ncol = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Original" = "#4C72B0", "Attritioned (Y26 Web Test)" = "#DD8452")) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(x = "Year 2 Externalising Symptoms", y = "Count") +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey90"),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

save_plot("11_attritioning_example", width  = 4, height = 6)

```

[View full-size plot](plots/pngs/11_attritioning_example.png){target="_blank"}
